zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry (Baron) de la Pomerai I於1110年出生在英國英格蘭戴文貝里 波梅洛易時，他的父親Joscelin de la Pomeraie36歲，母親Emma of Pomeroy20歲。他於1130年與Rohese Fitzroy Princess結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1164年在他的家鄉享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: (Baron)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the title of nobility, along the lines of Earl and Duke . The title arose in the Norman feudal system and seems to be of Germanic origin, derived from Old English beorn ‘young warrior’ or a related continental Germanic form. In part the given name, and in particular the variant Barron , may represent the surname derived from the title.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry (Baron) de la Pomerai I was born in 1110, in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Joscelin de la Pomeraie, was 36 and his mother, Emma of Pomeroy, was 20. He married Rohese Fitzroy Princess in 1130. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1164, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry (Baron) de la Pomerai I est né en 1110, à Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Joscelin de la Pomeraie, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Emma of Pomeroy, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Rohese Fitzroy Princess en 1130. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1164, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry (Baron) de la Pomerai Iが1110年にイギリスイギリスデボンバーリー ポメロイで生まれた時，父Joscelin de la Pomeraieは36歳，母Emma of Pomeroyは20歳であった。1130年にRohese Fitzroy Princessと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1164年に故郷で54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry (Baron) de la Pomerai I(이)가 1110년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 베리 폼로이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Joscelin de la Pomeraie(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma of Pomeroy(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1130년 Rohese Fitzroy Princess(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1164년 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry (Baron) de la Pomerai I родился в 1110 г. в местности бирри помирои, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Joscelin de la Pomeraie было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma of Pomeroy, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Rohese Fitzroy Princess в 1130 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1164 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.
